verdict: Moderate displacement risk. Wealth Manager scores 83 / 100 on AI displacement exposure over a 5-year horizon.

timeline.description: 2025: AI-powered wealth management platforms start to emerge.
2030: AI-driven investment recommendations become more prevalent.

key_tasks: Interview clients to determine their current income, expenses, insurance coverage, tax status, financial objectives, risk tolerance, or other information needed to develop a financial plan.
evidence_ledger: O*NET task list for SOC 13-2052.00 (Personal Financial Advisors)

reasoning_steps.description: High routine_cognitive score due to tasks involving financial planning and analysis.
reasoning_steps.impact: AI can already perform some aspects of wealth management.

reasoning_steps.description: Low creative_judgment score due to the emphasis on following established financial plans and strategies.
reasoning_steps.impact: Human advisors have less room for innovation in this field.

action_plan.description: Take courses in financial planning and analysis to improve skills.
action_plan.impact: Enhance job prospects for wealth management roles.

action_plan.description: Gain experience in data analysis and interpretation to complement financial planning skills.
action_plan.impact: Increase competitiveness in the job market.

follow_up_suggestions.name: Research emerging trends in wealth management and their impact on AI adoption.
follow_up_suggestions.description: Stay up-to-date with industry developments and adjust career plans accordingly.

follow_up_suggestions.name: Network with professionals in the wealth management field to learn about their experiences with AI tools.
follow_up_suggestions.description: Gain insights into the practical applications of AI in wealth management and potential opportunities for collaboration.

quotes.quote: AI is going to change every job. Every single job. Some jobs it will eliminate, some jobs it will improve, and some jobs it will create.
quotes.speaker: Jamie Dimon
quotes.title: CEO, JPMorgan Chase

quotes.quote: Two-thirds of jobs in the United States and Europe are exposed to some degree of AI automation, and roughly a quarter of current work could be done by generative AI.
quotes.speaker: Joseph Briggs and Devesh Kodnani
quotes.title: Economists, Goldman Sachs Research
quotes.source: Goldman Sachs report: The Potentially Large Effects of AI on Economic Growth

quotes.quote: Generative AI could add the equivalent of 2.6 to 4.4 trillion dollars in value annually across the global economy. Banking, high-tech, and life sciences capture the largest share.
quotes.speaker: McKinsey Global Institute
quotes.title: Research report
quotes.source: The Economic Potential of Generative AI

recent_developments.headline: Banks roll out AI copilots to analysts and traders
recent_developments.impact: JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, and Goldman now offer LLM tools to thousands of frontline staff.

recent_developments.headline: Audit automation hits routine reconciliation work
recent_developments.impact: Big-Four firms report AI handling first-pass review on a growing share of engagements.

recent_developments.headline: Robo-advice AUM continues climbing
recent_developments.impact: Algorithmic platforms now manage over $1 trillion globally, pressuring entry-level advisory roles.
